writing_points.claim: Dynamics and stability of the keyhole in full-field power-modulated laser welding unveiled by in-situ X-ray imaging
writing_points.source_text: Dynamics and stability of the keyhole in full-field power-modulated laser welding unveiled by in-situ X-ray imaging

writing_points.claim: • An energy density optimization for laser superposition regions improved keyhole stability and eliminated porosity.
writing_points.source_text: • Revealed keyhole dynamics during full-field power-modulated galvanometer laser welding using in-situ microfocus real-time X-ray imaging. • Key geometric features of the keyhole were extracted to quantitatively characterize its dynamic stability. • Mechanisms of circular, infinity, and 8-shaped trajectories affecting keyhole stability and porosity suppression were elucidated. • An energy density optimization for laser superposition regions improved keyhole stability and eliminated porosity.

writing_points.claim: Keywords: Full-field power-modulated laser Weld pool Keyhole In-situ X-ray imaging
writing_points.source_text: Keywords: Full-field power-modulated laser Weld pool Keyhole In-situ X-ray imaging

writing_points.claim: As a result, welding defects such as porosity and cracking are effectively suppressed.
writing_points.source_text: Full-field power-modulated laser welding is an emerging laser welding technology. It stabilizes the dynamic behavior of the keyhole by precisely regulating the spatiotemporal distribution of laser power. As a result, welding defects such as porosity and cracking are effectively suppressed. However, the large number of controllable process parameters, the difficulty in visualizing the keyhole, and the unclear mechanisms by which process parameters influence keyhole dynamics result in a lack of guidance in process optimization during practical welding applications. To address this issue, an in situ micro-focus X-ray real-time imaging system was used to directly monitor the dynamic behavior of the keyhole during laser welding of titanium alloy under different scanning trajectories and power modulation modes. Characteristic parameters, including depth vari­ ance, opening size, and depth-to-width ratio, were extracted. These parameters were combined with numerical simulation to analyze the effects of scanning trajectory and power modulation strategy on keyhole stability. The results indicate that, compared with conventional laser welding, the overall stability of the keyhole is signifi­ cantly enhanced under circular, infinity, and 8-shaped scanning trajectory modes, accompanied by a pronounced reduction in weld porosity. However, a small number of pores are still observed in regions where the laser trajectories superposition .This phenomenon is attributed to a sudden increase in keyhole depth within the trajectory superposition regions, which leads to reduced stability and even keyhole collapse. By adopting a control strategy that lowers the energy density in the laser trajectory superposition regions, the dynamic stability of the keyhole can be further improved, which effectively suppresses welding porosity.

writing_points.claim: As a result, a conventional Gaussian beam can be transformed into a customized beam that is tailored to meet diverse and complex welding requirements [1,2].
writing_points.source_text: Beam shaping is an emerging laser welding technology. It enables the modulation of the laser beam amplitude, phase, polarization state, and spot geometry through optical systems. As a result, a conventional Gaussian beam can be transformed into a customized beam that is tailored to meet diverse and complex welding requirements [1,2]. In the

writing_points.claim: field of laser welding, typical beam shaping strategies include oscillation laser welding, annular beam configurations, and full-field power- modulated lasers [3–5].
writing_points.source_text: field of laser welding, typical beam shaping strategies include oscillation laser welding, annular beam configurations, and full-field power- modulated lasers [3–5]. Such customized beam profiles demonstrate significant potential for overcoming technological bottlenecks in laser welding, effectively addressing critical issues such as excessive heat- affected zones and limited process windows. Consequently, beam shaping provides essential technical support for achieving high-quality,
